hypothesis: For graph datasets whose per-graph node-count distribution has excess kurtosis greater than one (e.g., AFLOW), dynamic batching yields a statistically different final test RMSE compared to static batching unless the optimizer learning rate is scaled proportionally to the mean number of real (non-padded) graphs per dynamic batch divided by the nominal batch size, in which case the final test RMSE will be statistically indistinguishable between the two batching methods.
